svg.aria-label: Control layers around an agent, ordered from hard to soft. The four outer hard layers are enforced by the harness: settings.json permissions.deny controls what the agent cannot see, PreToolUse hooks exiting 2 control what it cannot do, the tools allowlist controls what it cannot reach, and maxTurns is a circuit breaker. The two inner soft layers are advisory: the rules directory and the agent body. At the core sits the model, whose effort setting is a throttle rather than a rein.
svg.font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
svg.title: Control layers - reining the agents, from hard to soft
svg.text: Reining the agents - hard controls outside, soft controls inside
Outermost = enforced by the harness, the model gets no vote. Innermost = advisory. The ranking is the message.
HARD 1 settings.json permissions.deny -> what it cannot SEE
Read(**/.env), *.pem, *.key, id_rsa*, credentials.json, secrets/**, plus the Restricted data paths.
HARD 2 PreToolUse hooks, exit 2 -> what it cannot DO
Blocks: commit to main, bad commit message, edit to an Accepted ADR, read of .env. eval 15/15, model-independent.
HARD 3 tools: allowlist -> what it cannot REACH
Reviewers get Read, Grep, Glob, Bash - never Edit or Write. A reviewer that edits code is a dev agent.
HARD 4 maxTurns -> the circuit breaker
A stuck agent's cost is otherwise unbounded. debugger 30, code-reviewer 25, history-tracker 10.
SOFT 1 .claude/rules/ -> context, not configuration
Shapes behaviour; NOT a hard enforcement layer - it holds only if the model complies.
SOFT 2 the agent body / system prompt
The role, the scope, the procedure it is asked to follow. Still an instruction.
AGENT
the model. It gets a vote only inside the soft layers.
effort: a THROTTLE, not a rein - how hard it pulls, not where it can go
A soft control becomes hard the moment you can express it as a file the harness checks.
deterministic - enforced by the harness
advisory - the model decides
the model itself
